2026-04-17T09:35:10.4004485Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-17T09:35:10.4004929Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-17T09:35:10.4005302Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-17T09:35:10.4005613Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m

The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). The migration introduced:\\n\\n- **3 new resource types**: `DataBoxEdgeDeviceCapacityInfoResource`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceNetworkSettingsResource`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceUpdateSummaryResource`\\n- **New custom models**: `DataBoxEdgeDeviceCapacityInfo`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceNetworkSettings`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceUpdateSummary`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleAddonData`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleData`, `DataBoxEdgeTriggerData`, `EdgeFileEventTrigger`, `EdgeProfileSubscription`, `PeriodicTimerEventTrigger`\\n- **Significant API surface changes** to `DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleResource`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleAddonResource`, `DataBoxEdgeOrderResource`, and other existing resource types\\n- **Modified enum type**: `DataBoxEdgeEncryptionAlgorithm` and `DataBoxEdgeSkuName` were reworked (43 and 116 deletions respectively)\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **CHANGELOG `1.2.0-beta.1` section is empty** — the section exists but has no entries under \\u0026#34;Features Added\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;Breaking Changes\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;Bugs Fixed\\u0026#34;, or \\u0026#34;Other Changes\\u0026#34; despite substantial API additions and removals\\n- **README \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section is generic** — links only to external Azure SDK docs with no DataBoxEdge-specific content describing available resource types (`DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource`, `BandwidthScheduleResource`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleResource`, etc.)\\n- **README \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section has no code samples** — only links to a generic `(aka.ms/redacted) URL; no DataBoxEdge-specific snippets for common operations\\n- **New resource types are undocumented** — `DataBoxEdgeDeviceCapacityInfoResource`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceNetworkSettingsResource`, and `DataBoxEdgeDeviceUpdateSummaryResource` are not mentioned in README or CHANGELOG\\n- **No `Samples/` directory** in the tests folder — no test-backed snippet files exist at all\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/CHANGELOG.md` — **edit**\\n\\nUpdate the `1.2.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` section to document the changes introduced by the TypeSpec migration:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 1.2.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)\\n\\n### Features Added\\n\\n- Added new resource type `DataBoxEdgeDeviceCapacityInfoResource` exposing device capacity information.\\n- Added new resource type `DataBoxEdgeDeviceNetworkSettingsResource` exposing device network settings.\\n- Added new resource type `DataBoxEdgeDeviceUpdateSummaryResource` exposing device update summary.\\n- Added new model types: `DataBoxEdgeDeviceCapacityInfo`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceNetworkSettings`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceUpdateSummary`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleAddonData`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleData`, `DataBoxEdgeTriggerData`, `EdgeFileEventTrigger`, `EdgeProfileSubscription`, `PeriodicTimerEventTrigger`.\\n- Migrated the library code generation from AutoRest/Swagger to TypeSpec.\\n\\n### Breaking Changes\\n\\n- `DataBoxEdgeEncryptionAlgorithm` enum values have been updated to align with the TypeSpec-generated API.\\n- `DataBoxEdgeSkuName` enum values have been updated to align with the TypeSpec-generated API.\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\nReplace the generic \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; and \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; sections with DataBoxEdge-specific content.\\n\\nReplace the existing \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section body:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nKey concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nwith:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThe `Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge` library provides access to the following main resource types:\\n\\n- **`DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource`**: Represents a Data Box Edge physical device. Supports CRUD operations as well as actions such as scanning for updates, triggering updates, and retrieving security settings.\\n- **`BandwidthScheduleResource`**: Manages bandwidth schedules on a device to control network usage during specified time windows.\\n- **`DataBoxEdgeRoleResource`**: Manages compute roles (e.g., IoT, Kubernetes) running on the device.\\n- **`DataBoxEdgeRoleAddonResource`**: Manages add-ons associated with a role (e.g., Arc for Kubernetes).\\n- **`DataBoxEdgeShareResource`**: Manages data shares (SMB or NFS) on the device.\\n- **`DataBoxEdgeStorageAccountResource`**: Manages storage accounts configured on the device.\\n- **`DataBoxEdgeDeviceCapacityInfoResource`**: Provides information about device resource capacity utilization.\\n- **`DataBoxEdgeDeviceNetworkSettingsResource`**: Provides read access to the device\\u0026#39;s network configuration.\\n- **`DataBoxEdgeDeviceUpdateSummaryResource`**: Provides a summary of available updates for the device.\\n\\nFor general Azure Resource Manager SDK concepts, see the [Azure Resource Manager documentation]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nReplace the existing \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section body:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nCode samples for using the management library for .NET can be found in the following locations\\n- [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nwith:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nThe following sections show code snippets for common scenarios.\\n\\n### List all Data Box Edge devices in a subscription\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Get a specific Data Box Edge device\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### List bandwidth schedules for a device\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\nFor more samples, see the [Azure SDK for .NET management samples]((aka.ms/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate a new test file with sample code that the snippet tooling will inject into the README.\\n\\n#### New file: `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/tests/Samples/Sample1_DataBoxEdgeDevice.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_DataBoxEdgeDevice\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ListDevices()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:DataBoxEdge_ListDevices\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n\\n            await foreach (DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource device in subscription.GetDataBoxEdgeDevicesAsync())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Device: {device.Data.Name}, Location: {device.Data.Location}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task GetDevice()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:DataBoxEdge_GetDevice\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            DataBoxEdgeDeviceCollection devices = resourceGroup.GetDataBoxEdgeDevices();\\n            DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource device = await devices.GetAsync(\\u0026#34;myDataBoxEdgeDevice\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Device: {device.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;  Model: {device.Data.ModelDescription}\\u0026#34;);\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;  Status: {device.Data.DataBoxEdgeDeviceStatus}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ListBandwidthSchedules()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:DataBoxEdge_ListBandwidthSchedules\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource device = await resourceGroup.GetDataBoxEdgeDevices().GetAsync(\\u0026#34;myDataBoxEdgeDevice\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            await foreach (BandwidthScheduleResource schedule in device.GetBandwidthSchedules())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Schedule: {schedule.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**README snippet placeholder locations:**\\n\\n- Section: `## Examples` → `### List all Data Box Edge devices in a subscription`\\n  - Snippet name: `DataBoxEdge_ListDevices`\\n- Section: `## Examples` → `### Get a specific Data Box Edge device`\\n  - Snippet name: `DataBoxEdge_GetDevice`\\n- Section: `## Examples` → `### List bandwidth schedules for a device`\\n  - Snippet name: `DataBoxEdge_ListBandwidthSchedules`\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/README.md` currently has all required sections. After the edits above, verify:\\n\\n1. ✅ **Getting started** — present (Install, Prerequisites, Authenticate)\\n2. ⚠️ **Key concepts** — needs updating (replace generic link with service-specific content, as described in Step 1)\\n3. ⚠️ **Examples** — needs updating (add snippet placeholders and create sample test file, as described in Steps 1–2)\\n4. ✅ **Troubleshooting** — present\\n5. ✅ **Next steps** — present\\n6. ✅ **Contributing** — present\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 databoxedge` — injects snippet code from test file into README placeholders; check git diff to confirm README was updated\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 databoxedge` — not required unless public types were added beyond what the migration already exported\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Supports CRUD operations as well as actions such as scanning for updates, triggering updates, and retrieving security settings.\\n- **`BandwidthScheduleResource`**: Manages bandwidth schedules on a device to control network usage during specified time windows.\\n- **`DataBoxEdgeRoleResource`**: Manages compute roles (e.g., IoT, Kubernetes) running on the device.\\n- **`DataBoxEdgeRoleAddonResource`**: Manages add-ons associated with a role (e.g., Arc for Kubernetes).\\n- **`DataBoxEdgeShareResource`**: Manages data shares (SMB or NFS) on the device.\\n- **`DataBoxEdgeStorageAccountResource`**: Manages storage accounts configured on the device.\\n- **`DataBoxEdgeDeviceCapacityInfoResource`**: Provides information about device resource capacity utilization.\\n- **`DataBoxEdgeDeviceNetworkSettingsResource`**: Provides read access to the device\\u0026#39;s network configuration.\\n- **`DataBoxEdgeDeviceUpdateSummaryResource`**: Provides a summary of available updates for the device.\\n\\nFor general Azure Resource Manager SDK concepts, see the [Azure Resource Manager documentation]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nReplace the existing \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section body:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nCode samples for using the management library for .NET can be found in the following locations\\n- [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nwith:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nThe following sections show code snippets for common scenarios.\\n\\n### List all Data Box Edge devices in a subscription\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Get a specific Data Box Edge device\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### List bandwidth schedules for a device\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\nFor more samples, see the [Azure SDK for .NET management samples]((aka.ms/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate a new test file with sample code that the snippet tooling will inject into the README.\\n\\n#### New file: `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/tests/Samples/Sample1_DataBoxEdgeDevice.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_DataBoxEdgeDevice\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ListDevices()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:DataBoxEdge_ListDevices\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n\\n            await foreach (DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource device in subscription.GetDataBoxEdgeDevicesAsync())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Device: {device.Data.Name}, Location: {device.Data.Location}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task GetDevice()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:DataBoxEdge_GetDevice\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            DataBoxEdgeDeviceCollection devices = resourceGroup.GetDataBoxEdgeDevices();\\n            DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource device = await devices.GetAsync(\\u0026#34;myDataBoxEdgeDevice\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Device: {device.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;  Model: {device.Data.ModelDescription}\\u0026#34;);\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;  Status: {device.Data.DataBoxEdgeDeviceStatus}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ListBandwidthSchedules()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:DataBoxEdge_ListBandwidthSchedules\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource device = await resourceGroup.GetDataBoxEdgeDevices().GetAsync(\\u0026#34;myDataBoxEdgeDevice\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            await foreach (BandwidthScheduleResource schedule in device.GetBandwidthSchedules())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Schedule: {schedule.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**README snippet placeholder locations:**\\n\\n- Section: `## Examples` → `### List all Data Box Edge devices in a subscription`\\n  - Snippet name: `DataBoxEdge_ListDevices`\\n- Section: `## Examples` → `### Get a specific Data Box Edge device`\\n  - Snippet name: `DataBoxEdge_GetDevice`\\n- Section: `## Examples` → `### List bandwidth schedules for a device`\\n  - Snippet name: `DataBoxEdge_ListBandwidthSchedules`\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/README.md` currently has all required sections.
